prose: The Fukui reactivity calculation on benzaldehyde was performed using DFT at the B3LYP functional with the def2-TZVP basis set (tier 'standard') in the gas phase. The calculation was a single-point energy analysis on the geometry imported from the specified xyz file. The most electrophilic site (atom index 2, Carbon) has an f+ value of approximately 0.39939, indicating susceptibility to nucleophilic attack, while the most nucleophilic site (atom index 0, Oxygen) has an f- value of approximately 0.23840, indicating susceptibility to electrophilic attack. The calculation's integrity was verified as trustworthy, with some warnings about charge conservation suggesting large N±1 state deviations, but the results remain valid.
